title: FRMI News & Price Impact
markdown:
# Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) — News & Price-Impact Summary

**Window:** last 14 days (through 2026-07-16) · **Price:** ~$5.89 (−5.8% on the day; down ~25% over the window from ~$7.86 on July 2)
**Net read:** Bearish near-term. A $375M convertible-note offering announced July 9 reignited the market's core fear about this pre-revenue story — dilution and financing risk — and knocked the stock down another leg to fresh lows. The governance war that drove June's volatility has quieted (the founder's proxy campaign is suspended), removing the speculative sale-process upside, while the one catalyst that could re-rate the stock — a signed hyperscaler power lease at Project Matador — remains unsigned. Cash is burning fast and the shares now sit near their 52-week low.

## What's moving the stock

The dominant driver this window is a **capital raise the market read as a signal of stress.** On **July 9, 2026**, Fermi announced a proposed **$350 million offering of convertible senior notes due 2031** (with a $52.5M over-allotment option), placed privately to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A. The deal was **upsized and priced at $375 million with a 5.00% coupon**, maturing July 15, 2031, convertible at the company's election into cash, stock, or a combination. Fermi said it would use part of the proceeds to buy **capped-call transactions to offset dilution**, with the rest for general corporate purposes.

The reaction was immediate and negative: FRMI fell roughly **14% in after-hours trading** on the announcement and closed down about **10-14% across July 9-10**, shedding on the order of half a billion dollars of market value. The mechanism is straightforward and is the whole reason the stock is a battleground — Fermi is **pre-revenue and burning cash at an extraordinary rate** (a Q1 2026 net loss of roughly **−$189M** and free cash flow near **−$448M** for the quarter). A convertible raise tells the market two things at once: the company needs outside capital to fund the Project Matador build-out, and that capital comes with **future equity dilution** baked in (converts turn into shares if the stock recovers). The capped-call hedge softens but does not eliminate that dilution, and for a story whose entire valuation rests on a *future* event, any confirmation that the balance sheet can't self-fund the plan is taken hard. The stock has now round-tripped from late-June closes near $9-10 down to **~$5.89**, sitting just above its 52-week low of $4.47 (versus a 52-week high of $36.99).

Layered underneath is the **governance overhang**, which has cooled but not vanished. Founder, largest shareholder, and former CEO **Toby Neugebauer** spent the spring waging a proxy campaign to replace the board and force a banker-led "dual-track" strategic review of Matador (a sale-versus-lease auction to crystallize value). On **July 3** he **suspended that campaign** after the presiding Texas Business Court judge abruptly recused himself just before a key hearing, claiming ~70% of recorded votes had backed his special-meeting bid; the company subsequently declared that shareholders had rejected the founder's proposal. He has vowed to keep fighting through the courts — including a challenge to a board bylaw raising the shareholder-vote supermajority threshold to 70%, on which a judge granted **expedited discovery** on June 26. For the stock, the practical effect is that the near-term "forced sale unlocks value" optionality that fed June's rally has drained away, leaving the shares to trade on fundamentals — and the fundamentals right now are cash burn and a capital raise.

Net: the convertible offering is the fresh, stock-specific catalyst driving the decline; the quieted proxy fight removed a speculative support; and the bull case (a marquee AI tenant) is still pending. That combination is why FRMI made new lows this window.

## Other developments

**Analyst targets remain wildly split — a sign nobody can price this yet.** Street price targets span from **UBS's low of $8** to **Citizens' $30**, with a median around **$17** — i.e., even the *most bearish* published target sits above the current ~$5.89, yet the range itself (nearly 4x low-to-high) tells you the outcome is binary and unknowable until a tenant signs. The wide dispersion is the story: this is a bet on landing a hyperscaler, not a debate about next-quarter margins.
— stockstotrade / gurufocus (Tier 2, opinion) · 2026-07-10 · analyst rating / valuation · mixed (low conf.) · context, not a discrete move

**Insider selling adds to the cautious tape.** Over the trailing three months insiders have sold roughly **$7.3M** of stock with **no insider buying** reported. On its own this is modest and not necessarily a directional call, but against a falling price and a dilutive raise it does nothing to reassure holders looking for an insider vote of confidence.
— gurufocus / stockstotrade (Tier 2) · ~2026-07-10 · insider / ownership · bearish (low conf.) · noise-to-low impact

**Filtered as noise:** the recurring "Is FRMI undervalued / should you buy" listicles (Simply Wall St, StockInvest, Motley-Fool-style pieces) and recycled quote-page reprints. Seen and set aside — interpretation, not events.

## Quick reference

| Date | Headline | Impact |
|------|----------|--------|
| 2026-07-16 | Shares slide to ~$5.89, near 52-week low | CONFIRMED −5.8% (day) / ~−25% (window) |
| 2026-07-10 | Convertible-offering fallout continues; weak financials pressure bulls | CONFIRMED (part of the −10-14% July 9-10 de-rating) |
| 2026-07-09 | Proposed $350M (priced/upsized $375M, 5.00%, due 2031) convertible notes | CONFIRMED −14% after-hours |
| 2026-07-03 | Neugebauer suspends proxy campaign after judge's recusal (claims ~70% of votes) | governance overhang / removed speculative upside |
| 2026-06-26 | Court grants expedited discovery on 70% supermajority bylaw | ANTICIPATED (litigation live) |

## What to watch

Near-term the trade is bearish: a dilutive raise into heavy cash burn has pushed the stock to new lows with the governance catalyst spent. The single most important thing to watch is still a **signed anchor tenant at Project Matador** — that is the binary re-rating event on which the entire valuation gap (~$5.89 vs. $8-30 targets) rests.

- **A signed Project Matador power lease / hyperscaler tenant** — timing unknown; management has hinted "soon" but nothing is executed. This is the one event that flips the story.
- **Q2 earnings — expected around 2026-07-28** — watch the cash-burn trajectory, remaining runway after the $375M raise, dilution math, and any capacity-delivery timeline update.
- **The Neugebauer litigation** — the 70%-supermajority bylaw challenge and expedited discovery remain live in the Texas courts; a ruling could revive the sale-process narrative and its speculative upside.
- **AI-power sentiment** (OpenAI / Nvidia data-center capacity headlines) — FRMI trades as a high-beta proxy for hyperscaler power demand, so sector news can move it independent of company-specific catalysts.

4. StocksToTrade — "FRMI Stock Slides As Cash Burn And Debt Weigh On Outlook" / "Weak Financials Pressure Bulls" (2026-07-10): Q1 net loss −$188.7M, FCF −$448.5M, targets UBS $8 / Citizens $30 / median $17, insider sales $7.3M. https://stockstotrade.com/news/fermi-inc-frmi-news-2026_07_10/

*Not investment advice. Price attribution is inferential — it links headlines to observed moves but cannot prove causation. Price data is end-of-day/delayed; verify anything you act on against primary sources (SEC EDGAR, company IR).*
